Deep readiness — actually touches the DB.

post.description: Always inserts. Never updates. That's the contract.

schema.title: Response Append Parameters Api User Parameters Post

get.description:
Two query modes combined:

- `latest=true`: one row per key, the most recent. Used by the app to resolve prescriptions.
- `key=X` [+ `since=Y`]: full history for a key (Claude's analytical queries).

Without `latest` or `key`, returns all rows for the user in chronological order.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Required — scopes the query to one user.
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Required — scopes the query to one user.

parameters.name: latest

schema.description: If true, return latest-per-key for the user.
schema.title: Latest

parameters.description: If true, return latest-per-key for the user.

parameters.name: key

schema.description: Filter to a single key (for history queries).
schema.title: Key

parameters.description: Filter to a single key (for history queries).

ExerciseLastPerformed.title: ExerciseLastPerformed
ExerciseLastPerformed.description: Inline history the app uses to show 'last time you did this exercise.'

ExerciseUpsert.title: ExerciseUpsert
ExerciseUpsert.description: Claude owns the id. Upsert-by-id: same id updates, new id inserts.

SyncResultsIn.title: SyncResultsIn
SyncResultsIn.description: App pushes this when a workout finishes (or on next connectivity).

WorkoutCreate.title: WorkoutCreate
WorkoutCreate.description: Accepts a full workout tree (blocks → items → alternatives) in one request.

WorkoutStatusUpdate.title: WorkoutStatusUpdate
WorkoutStatusUpdate.description: App tells server 'this workout transitioned to status X at time Y.'

WorkoutUpdate.title: WorkoutUpdate
WorkoutUpdate.description: Partial update. Blocks, if present, replace the full tree (simpler than diff).
